channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Police nab eight suspected cultists over assault on NDU student in Bayelsa
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bayelsa State Police have arrested eight suspected cultists for allegedly assaulting an NDU student over a N70,000 debt. Investigations into Bayelsa cultis
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-nab-eight-suspected-cultists-over-assault-on-ndu-student-in-bayelsa/

item.title: Anambra teacher accused of flogging 12-year-old boy to death surrenders to police
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Anambra teacher accused of flogging a 12-year-old boy to death has surrendered to police. Read more about the ongoing investigation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/anambra-teacher-accused-of-flogging-12-year-old-boy-to-death-surrenders-to-police/

item.title: Borno commissioner shares 15-second video of kidnapped students at summit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Borno State confirms receiving a 15-second proof-of-life video of students abducted from Lassa. The government is working to secure the release of the Born
Read More: https://punchng.com/borno-commissioner-shares-15-second-video-of-kidnapped-students-at-summit/

item.title: ‎Debt Claim: Timi Dakolo Denies Owing £4,106, Threatens Legal Action Against DJ Timmy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎Soul singer Timi Dakolo has denied allegations that he owes Revolve Entertainment £4,106 in expenses tied to a proposed United Kingdom tour, with his lawyers threatening legal action against the promoter and its representative, Otis Ayodele Kubeyinje, known as Baddest DJ Timmy. ‎In a letter dated J

item.title: Huge rise in harmful social media posts this World Cup
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: There have been 14 times more harmful social media posts and comments during this World Cup than in 2022, Fifa finds.

item.title: Russian online retail warehouses hit by deadly Ukrainian strikes
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Drones targeted Wildberries facilities near Moscow and in Tambov. Ukraine's leader called them "major logistics facilities" supplying "sanctioned components".

item.title: 2027: El-Rufai, Ego Wars and the Dwindling Fortunes of the ADC
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: By Jamilu Isa Kwarbai Forget the grand talk of “rescuing Nigerian democracy.” The African Democratic Congress (ADC) is learning the hard way that a political movement cannot capture the presidency

item.title: Senator urges Navy to intensify efforts to protect Nigeria’s waterways
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The senator representing Cross River southern senatorial district, Asuquo Ekpenyong jr has called on the Nigerian Navy to intensify efforts to curb increasing maritime crimes in the South-South region, which is largely coastal. Ekpenyong tasked the Navy protect Nigeria’s waterways. The lawmake

item.title: Countdown begins as Burna Boy, Shakira gear up for historic World Cup moment
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigerian Afrobeats superstar Burna Boy and Colombian pop icon Shakira have reunited ahead of Sunday's FIFA World Cup final, sharing a memorable moment during rehearsals at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, United States.

item.title: Investors Propose $145m Solar Manufacturing Plant In Abia
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: An indigenous company, MD Nwakanma Nigeria Limited, has proposed a $145 million solar manufacturing plant in Abia State under a Public-Private Partnership (PPP) arrangement with the state government. The proposed facility will be located at Umuelele in Isiala Ngwa South Local Government Area. Speaki

item.title: Indian police shift Cockroach Party activist on hunger strike to hospital against his wish
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Indian police on Saturday forcibly moved environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk to hospital after his health deteriorated during a weeks-long hunger strike in New Delhi. Wangchuk, a prominent activist tied to the viral, opposition Cockroach Janata Party, is seeking education reforms. His family has denounc

item.title: The classroom moment that bonds finalists De la Fuente & Scaloni forever
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Spain manager Luis de la Fuente and Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni will go head-to-head in the World Cup final on Sunday - and the two men know each other well.

item.title: DeChambeau 'made himself the story' - Bryson's rules controversy explained
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Bryson DeChambeau has been criticised for ‘making himself the story’ by the way he handled a two-shot penalty during his second round at The Open.

item.title: Iran accuses US of striking critical infrastructure as war intensifies
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The conflict has been escalating since US President Donald Trump declared 10 days ago the peace deal with Iran is over.

item.title: No FCT Community Will Be Left Behind – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Friday reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to extending quality infrastructure to satellite towns and underserved communities in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), declaring that no community would be left behind. Represented by the Speaker of the House of Represent

item.description: Ukrainian strikes hit Russian logistics center

item.title: Borno commissioner shares video of kidnapped students at summit
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Borno State Government on Saturday shared a proof-of-life video showing some of the students kidnapped from Government Day Secondary School, Lassa, in Askira/Uba Local Government Area. The video was presented by the Commissioner for Education, Science and Technology, Abba Wakilbe, during the Bor

item.title: Olu Of Warri, Queen Consort Launch Initiative To Promote Itsekiri Identity
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Olu of Warri, Ogiame Atuwatse III, and the Queen Consort, Olori Atuwatse III, have unveiled a cultural initiative aimed at strengthening the identity, unity and global visibility of the Itsekiri people. The initiative, known as “I Am Itsekiri,” will be launched under the authority of the Palace

item.title: Quiz: Name every men's World Cup final host city
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: The World Cup final is where heroes are made and dreams come true for players and fans alike. Can you name every city that has ever hosted the showpiece?

item.title: Imo Assembly Condemns Continued Extension Of Acting Chief Judge’s Tenure
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Imo State House of Assembly has condemned the continued extension of the tenure of the Acting Chief Judge of the state, Justice Ijeoma Agugua, by the National Judicial Council (NJC), describing the practice as unconstitutional. The position of the Assembly followed a motion of urgent public impo

item.title: Loud Urban Choir Marks Growth Through Music, Live Performances
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Loud Urban Choir is a Nigerian contemporary choral group that incorporates elements of Afrobeats, gospel, R&B, and contemporary popular music into its performances. Since its formation in 2019, the ensemble has experienced growth in its activities through digital performances, original recording

item.title: Lebanon’s president departs for Washington to meet Trump
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: It will be the first trip to Washington by a Lebanese head of state since Michel Sleiman was received by Barack Obama in 2009. The post Lebanon’s president departs for Washington to meet Trump appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Ex-NNPP boss Odeyemi appointed DLA Acting chairman in Osun
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Democratic Leadership Alliance (DLA) has appointed Dr Odeyemi Tosin as the Acting Chairman of the party in Osun State, with immediate effect. The post Ex-NNPP boss Odeyemi appointed DLA Acting chairman in Osun appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Your Social Security check could go up by $74 a month next year
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Social Security’s cost-of-living-adjustment could be 3.8% in 2027, as inflation shows signs of cooling.

item.title: ‘Don’t stop at Malami’ Aisha Yesufu points EFCC to bigger targets
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Prominent activist and co-convener of the Bring Back Our Girls movement, Aisha Yesufu, has called on the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) to widen its anti-corruption investigations to include other public officials facing allegations of financial misconduct.

item.title: Otu declares war on insecurity with Anti-Terror Campaign
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Cross River State Governor, Senator Bassey Otu, has declared war on insecurity, launching a statewide “Unite Against Terror” campaign as government moves to confront terrorism, kidnapping, cultism and other emerging threats head-on. The post Otu declares war on insecurity with Anti-Terror Campaign a

item.title: Hamilton crashes out as Antonelli sets pace in final Belgium practice
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lewis Hamilton's Ferrari crew face a race against time to repair his car for Belgian Grand Prix Qualifying after he crashed at the end of final practice, in a session topped again by Mercedes' title-leading Kimi Antonelli.

item.title: Cybercrime: Rivers Police clampdown on authors of viral market-women-kidnappers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Rivers State Police Command has debunked a viral audio recording and an accompanying photograph circulating on social media, of market-women-kidnappers, describing the claims of an attempted kidnapping along Woji Bridge in Port Harcourt, as false and misleading. The post Cybercrime: Rivers Polic

item.title: Peter Obi visits Amaechi [VIDEO]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria Democratic Congress, ADC, presidential candidate, Peter Obi, has paid a solidarity visit to the presidential running mate of the African Democratic Congress, ADC, Rotimi Amaechi, over the death of his mother, Mary Oduah. Obi visited Amaechi and his wife, Dame Judith, in what could be seen as

item.title: Buhari-era military rivalry denied troops air support, says Bwala
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Presidential aide Daniel Bwala has alleged that a rivalry between the Nigerian Army and the Nigerian Air Force during former President Muhammadu Buhari’s administration resulted in troops being denied air support during some military operations. The post Buhari-era military rivalry denied troops air

item.title: Court Dismisses Bid To Amend APC Primary Suit, Adjourns Magama/Rijau Case To July 22
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The suit was instituted by APC aspirant, Shehu Auna, through his attorney, Abubakar Danjuma, challenging the validity of the party's primary election conducted on June 16, 2026, which returned Yakubu Auna as the party's flag bearer for the constituency. The post Court Dismisses Bid To Amend APC Prim

item.title: Electricity: Blackout hits six Nigerian states
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Six states in northern Nigeria will experience blackout on Saturday and Sunday. The states are Bauchi, Gombe, Yobe (Damaturu), Borno (Maiduguri), Adamawa (Yola), and Taraba (Jalingo). Spokesperson of Transmission Company of Nigeria, TCN, Ndidi Mbah, made this known in a statement on Saturday. TCN sa

item.title: IPAC to conduct CVR mop-up campaign in Cross River
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Inter-Party Advisory Council (IPAC) in Cross River State has announced plans to intensify a last-minute grassroots mobilisation campaign to encourage eligible residents to participate in the ongoing Continuous Voter Registration (CVR) exercise before it closes. item.title: Sony’s flagship RGB LED TV is incredible
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The Sony Bravia 9 II is the most anticipated new TV in years. It's an amazing RGB LED TV. I watched Dungeons & Dragons: Honor Among Thieves on the new Bravia with my son, who has been getting into the roleplaying game but had never seen the movie. The landscapes of Faerûn looked natural and

Oshodi and Justice Okunuga in Charge No. ID/25771C/2025 and Charge No. ID/25769C/2025 for his arraignment and to defend the charges if he believes that he did

item.title: For years, farmers suffered alone in farming, says FG
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Okwe Obi, Abuja The Federal Government has disclosed that for years, farmers bore the brunt of cultivation without meaningful assistance. The government stated that the biggest shift is not the volume of inputs, but the structure of agricultural delivery. Minister of Agriculture and Food Securi

item.title: DJ Timmy calls out Timi Dakolo over alleged debt, threatens to expose ‘sexcapades’
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian disc jockey, Kubeyinje Otis Ayodeji, professionally known as Baddest DJ Timmy, has called out singer Timi Dakolo over alleged debt. The disc jockey who called the singer out in an Instagram post on Friday, explained that Dakolo had a month ago pleaded with him to take down his earlier post

item.title: US-Iran fighting escalates with infrastructure targeted
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iran on Saturday struck infrastructure in the Gulf and threatened a "full-scale offensive" in retaliation for the seventh consecutive night of US strikes on the Islamic republic. The post US-Iran fighting escalates with infrastructure targeted appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Trabzonspor Demand €30m For Onuachu
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Trabzonspor have slapped a €30 million price tag on Paul Onuachu amid transfer interest, Completesports.com reports. The Nigeria international has been strongly linked with a move away from Papara Park this summer. Trabzonspor have rejected two separate offers from Fenerbahçe. Fenerbahçe’s Ist

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Federal Government has denied reports that it plans to stop the export of raw cocoa beans.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Bishop Ande Emmanuel has appealed to members of breakaway factions within The United Methodist Church to embrace reconciliation and return to the church, describing unity as essential to the fulfilment of its mission and the strengthening of the Christian faith. The appeal comes amid ongoing discuss

item.title: Kano Assembly, KSCHMA partner on healthcare financing reforms
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Kano State House of Assembly and the Kano State Contributory Healthcare Management Agency (KSCHMA) have strengthened their collaboration on healthcare financing reforms with the convening of a one-day technical stakeholders’ workshop aimed at improving the state’s contributory health

item.title: In fairness to Umahi, By Osmund Agbo
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: David Nweze Umahi, a two-term former governor of Ebonyi State, former senator of the Federal Republic, and Nigeria’s current Minister of Works, is no stranger to controversy. In fact, controversy seems to dog his every step. He is also not a public figure known for shying away from wielding au

item.description: This week France 24's media show "Scoop" looks at Donald Trump's latest attempt to intimidate the press. item.description: Bendel Insurance have completed the signing of forward, Suraj Lawal, DAILY POST reports. The Benin-Arsenal announced Lawal’s arrival at an unveiling ceremony in Benin City. Lawal joined Bendel Insurance on a one-year contract. The energetic forward was on the books of Abia Warriors last season

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is considering firing Armed Forces commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi, the Financial Times reported, following days of turmoil engulfing the country’s military leadership.

item.description: The match no one wants to play kicks off on Saturday in Miami. Kyl

item.description: The Niger State Ministry of Health has confirmed a suspected outbreak of diphtheria in the Fadikpe area of Minna, with one child already dead, one recovered and two others receiving treatment, as health authorities intensify efforts to contain the disease. item.title: More games should be on rails (literally)
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: It's been a good few weeks for games on rails. Nintendo's Star Fox remake wisely kept the tightly scripted, action-packed levels from Star Fox 64 largely the same, and they're still fun to fly through nearly 20 years later. Denshattack!, a new game from Undercoders, similarly features levels packed

item.title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 136, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been recording the next season of V

item.title: How a homegrown Chinese chip maker became the memory industry’s biggest wild card
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: As ChangXin Memory Technologies gears up for a breakout public listing, the chip maker is eyeing the turf held by Micron, SK Hynix and Samsung.

item.title: Forget gasoline: Why the price surge in this under-the-radar fuel is the real threat to the U.S. economy
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: As investors fret about gasoline, diesel prices have been rising and are threatening to cause even more economic destruction.

item.title: This may be the real reason USDA made a huge revision to its beef-export sales data
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: It’s ‘like driving one more nail into the proverbial coffin,’ says a rancher about the recent deterioration in U.S. agriculture data

item.title: House passes Common Cents Act to help with phasing out the penny. How cash purchases would work if the bill becomes law
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Not Done Yet Refunding Tariffs or Realizing AI’s Promise
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The markets continue to be dominated by news of artificial intelligence

item.title: 1xCup produces stars as organisers unveil bigger talent pathway for 2026 tournament
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Organisers of Nigeria's leading grassroots football competition, 1xCup, have unveiled an ambitious roadmap for the 2026 edition, positioning the tournament not merely as a football championship but as a professional talent pipeline after revealing that several former participants have already secure

item.title: Super Eagles invitee set to leave Germany and Italy for Turkey
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles invitee Gift Orban is closing in on another move with the Nigerian striker set to join newly promoted Turkish Süper Lig club Amedspor on a season-long loan from Bundesliga side TSG Hoffenheim, Soccernet.ng reports. The transfer has reached an advanced stage after Hoffenheim and Amedspor

item.title: Canada wildfires affect air quality before World Cup final
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: More than 800 wildfires are burning across Canada, including around 190 in northern Ontario, with the surge in fires largely attributed to climate change. Thick smoke has created hazardous air quality across parts of Canada and several US states, including Minnesota, Illinois, Michigan and New York.

item.title: The S&P 500 is breaking the earnings playbook: Chart of the Day
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Big banks' record Wall Street profits are increasingly tied to AI
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance
